Data Export Approval & DPA Schedule – Seri Banyan Industries Berhad (Reg. No. SBIB-2020-001), a Bursa Malaysia-listed issuer, effective 02 June 2026.
Parties and roles: Data User – Seri Banyan Industries Berhad; Data Processor – Awan Lintas Cloud Sdn Bhd; Approved Sub-processor – NimbusBeta Pte Ltd (for support services only).
PDPA scope: “Personal Data” and “Sensitive Personal Data” as defined under the Malaysian PDPA 2010; processing limited to HR onboarding, payroll support, and employee helpdesk functions, with no processing for marketing.
Data minimisation policy (onboarding): collect essential fields only; NRIC/Passport last 4 digits, masked bank account reference, corporate email, role and cost centre; no collection of political beliefs or health data for onboarding.
Cross-border transfer approval: limited export of HR ticket metadata to NimbusBeta Pte Ltd (Singapore) for incident triage is approved under PDPA s.129 on consent and contractual necessity; DTA ref: SBIB-ALC-24; encryption in transit and at rest; audit trail retained by the Data User.
Transfer boundaries: no onward international transfers beyond the approved Singapore support environment; no analytics lake ingestion or marketing use; sandbox data auto-pseudonymised with token vault in MY region.
Retention controls: HR onboarding records retained for 7 years post-cessation or shorter if required by law; helpdesk logs for 24 months; quarterly purge jobs scheduled per Retention Schedule SB-RET-07 with system-generated evidence.
Consent management: individuals may withdraw consent for non-mandatory processing by emailing privacy@seribanyan.example; withdrawal does not affect statutory or contractual retention requirements.
DSAR workflow: access/correction requests via portal.seribanyan.example/dsar with standard 21-day response; there are no outstanding DSARs or erasure requests as at the date of this note.
Security and access: role-based access controls, least privilege, keys held in MY–KL region; no privileged administrator accounts located outside Malaysia; telemetry excludes personal identifiers.
Bursa/MNPI statement: this schedule contains no earnings guidance, deal terms, or other material non-public information; any corporate developments referenced are already disclosed on Bursa LINK and remain subject to the Listing Requirements.
Insider compliance context: staff remain bound by generic blackout training under the CMSA; this note does not specify blackout windows, price-sensitive triggers, or insider lists.
Governance closure: Vendor due diligence and DPIA are closed under record DPO-2026-09 with no open corrective actions; queries to dpo@seribanyan.example.
